title: Phenomenon 35-A
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Phenomenon 35-A: Jovial Syndrome}}{{User:JakitoFan/disappearingcollapse|initial={{Warning|content=images and vivid descriptions of gore.}}
!! EPILEPSY WARNING !!


The following page contains flashy visuals and CSS which may cause an epileptic seizure. If you are affected by Photosensitive Epilepsy or related conditions, read this at your own risk. It is suggested you click off this page.


You have been warned.
|num=Epilepsywarning|body=}}{{Background|file=Discolight.gif}}{{VoidedTheme}}{{blurimage|file=SFV.jpg|caption=An illustration of a Jovial}}{{Audio|file=Calamity Fortune.mp3|volume=0.05}} __NOTOC__ == Description == '''Phenomenon 35-A''', better known as '''Jovial Syndrome''', and those affected by it being known as '''Entity 67-A''' or '''Jovials'''Prior to '''Jovial Syndrome''' being identified as a separate condition, the term "jovial" was also used to refer to regular SFV victims and Partygoers, hence why the [[Party Crashers]] were originally referred to as the "Anti-Jovial Militia", is a rare condition affecting those infected by the [[Sanguine Festivus]] virus. Its main symptom is an uncontrollable, permanent feeling of happiness at all times, and limited retainment and even recovery of memories from pre infection. Due to this, it has been widely seen as the opposite of the [[Phenomenon 35|wretched cycle]], which has proven this somewhat true, as both target the brain and cause overwhelming emotion to override critical judgement. Unlike its more famous counterpart, there is no reliable way of predicting who will be subjected to this phenomenon, other than being infected with [[Sanguine Festivus]] and not being inside of [[Level Fun/The Party Rooms|level fun]].Partygoers, for unknown reasons, are immune to '''Jovial Syndrome''', with the exception of the [[The Co-Hosts|Co-Hosts]]. It is unknown why, but some believe that [[Caius Taggert]] monitors victims of the virus he deems useful to him, and uses his control over the virus to initiate '''Jovial Syndrome''' artificially, in order to preserve their intellect. What is known is that it is exceedingly rare, with only around one in 500 victims of the virus ever undergoing '''Jovial Syndrome'''. Due to this rarity, many victims believe themselves to be blessed and rewarded for past actions, giving them a sense of superiority over other beings. This narrative is especially popular with [[The Halloween Party]], who claim that those with the condition have been personally selected by God to be given a second chance, should they abide by his wishes to punish the other humans of the backrooms for their sins. This has worked extremely well for the group, and is one of the main reasons that '''Jovials''' in the group exhibit no mercy, despite their painful, and often sadistic, killing sprees: they genuinely believe that they are superior to all others. == Stages of infection == === Stages One and Two === In these stages, the virus behaves as normal, and it is currently impossible to tell if the victim will develop '''Jovial Syndrome''' or not. See [[Sanguine Festivus]] for more information on symptoms and treatments during these early stages. === Stage Three === During this time, symptoms '''Jovial Syndrome''' will reveal themselves. This point is not triggered after a specific amount of time, but rather when the victim is at their lowest in terms of their mental state. The victim will feel utterly starved, both of affection and in a literal sense. And then, just as they believe all to be lost, a surge of virus activity occurs throughout the limbic system, causing one to be overcome with feelings of joy and accomplishment. The hippocampus also undergoes a surge of activity, allowing one to recover some of their pre infection memories.At this point, the victim is considered an instance of '''Entity 67-A''' This does not diminish the appetite typical of [[Sanguine Festivus|SFV]] carriers. If anything, it enhances it. Whenever a '''Jovial''' bites, kills, or consumes a human or human flesh, they experience a huge surge of dopamine. This is believed to be a feature of the virus to infect more human cells. The carriers are far less likely to fall into depression and give up on their hunting if they have an incentive besides delaying the inevitable return of pain and starvation. And indeed, '''Jovials''' generally exhibit much higher kill counts than their Partygoer and standard victim counterparts. Regardless of purpose, in almost every case, victims of '''Jovial Syndrome''' have developed addictions to killing and cannibalism that have plagued victims even after they've been cured, which is still possible at this stage if one seeks immediate medical attention. If cured at stage three, victims normally have withdrawal symptoms from a lack of bloodshed, even though they no longer experience the rush of dopamine they did whilst infected. Unfortunately, this has led to a few survivors of '''Jovial Syndrome''' to become serial killers while human. === Stage Four === {{Blurimage|align=right|file=SFV-Victim-Photo.png|caption=Photo of an encounter with a '''Jovial''' in [[Level Fun/The Party Rooms|Level Fun]], presumably developed '''Jovial Syndrome''' prior to entering the level.}} At this stage, there is almost no potential for the victim to be cured. It is at this point, usually a few months after the beginning of stage three, that the bones and muscles around the face and skull will shift and grow and contort their facial expression. It is a myth that the mouths of '''Jovials''' are forced into a permanent smile. In fact, the jaw simply extends in the direction one arranged their mouth in, meaning that rate neutral faced or frowning '''Jovials''' do exist. As this stage persists, the mouth and cheeks begin to split open far wider than should be natural for a human. The victims feel surprisingly little pain from this ordeal, describing it as similar to the sensation one gets when losing their baby teeth. In incredibly rare instances, victims have been saved from this stage, but the methods used are incredibly unreliable and more likely to just kill the unfortunate victim. === Stage Five === The existence of stage five is currently unconfirmed, but serves as a possible explanation to the appearance and abilities of the most famous '''Jovial''', [[Caius Taggert]]. If true, stage five occurs approximately 90 years post infection. The main physical symptom is further drastic structural changes of the skeleton, starting with growth through several points of the skull to form a natural bone helmet, followed by a similar second layer of bone all around the body. The victim will grow a second layer of ribs that lie above the skin, as well as a second layer of shoulder blades and kneecaps. It is thought that they also gain access to some of Taggert's abilities, though which are caused by the virus and which are abilities from himself is unclear. The source of this information comes from detained '''Jovials''' and eyewitness accounts, but all lack proof and in all cases contain contradictory information (symptoms explained here are mostly agreed on), so take all information on this hypothetical stage with a grain of salt. If it exists, the only people known to live to reach stage five are Taggert himself and [[Joseph Butcher]], who is thought to have started showing early symptoms of this stage just prior to his death. While it cannot be stressed enough that this stage's existence is not confirmed, there is one proven symptom '''Jovials''' gain as they age, that being the gradual decline of the constant happiness sensation. While it never fully disappears, it does give older '''Jovials''' more time to think to themselves, usually thoughts on their next meal. {| class="fandom-table" |+
Notable infected individuals
! !Notes |- |[[Caius Taggert]] |The most famous and most powerful '''Jovial'''. Has gained the ability to somehow control the [[Sanguine Festivus]] virus itself, among other inexplicable abilities. |- |[[The Co-Hosts]] |The only Partygoers to also become '''Jovial Partygoers'''.A condition unique to the Co-Hosts and Caius Taggert, in which one is simultaneously a '''Jovial''' and a partygoer. Also much more intelligent than the average '''Jovial'''. |- |[[The Party Pooper]] and [[Clyde Nelson#Mercy Delcroix|Mercy Delcroix]] |Both of these individuals are the only ones known to be able to prevent the endless happiness. Like the Co-Hosts, they are also more intelligent than most '''Jovials'''. Both have also taken measures to hide their disease out of embarrassment. Wilson has taken to wearing a mask while Delcroix stitched the wider areas of her mouth shut. |- |[[Joseph Butcher]] |The only '''Jovial''' other than Taggert known to live past 100. |- |[[Joy]] |An associate of Taggert's army.Technically not a part of it, but she holds close relations with them. She rarely takes part in their conflicts, opting to mostly work alone.She can imitate weaker versions of some of his abilities, including limited control of the virus. She also has above average speed and strength compared to the rest of her kind, especially for her young age. |- |[[Kyle Thorns]] |Another associate of Caius Taggert. Interestingly, he is showing signs of stage five, namely thorn-like bones protruding from his skin, despite being far younger than than both Taggert and Butcher. |- |} == Telepathic Suggestion == Unlike the [[Sanguine Festivus|rest of their kind]], the souls of '''Jovials''' are not banished to [[The Afterparty]], making it impossible to mind control them in the same way [[Caius Taggert]] controls the rest of his forces. They can, however, receive unconscious telepathic messages that make them more inclined to believe certain things and follow certain people. It is very much possible to resist this, but with the weakening mind of a '''Jovial''', it is unsurprising that many of them fall victim to it, and lose their freedom to it. Caius Tagget is currently the only person known to possess such a power through purely himself, though the pentagram necklaces of [[The Halloween Party#The lords of Hell|the Lords of Hell]] allow them to induce such an effect artificially, hence why [[Caius Taggert|The Partygoer Jamboree]]As stated earlier, the Partygoer transformation SFV victims go through in [[Level Fun/The Party Rooms|Level Fun]] usually overrides that of '''Jovial Syndrome''', so most of the Jamboree's '''Jovials''' are sourced from infected victims from outside the level, as the Partygoer transformation has no effect on anyone who enters the level already having '''Jovial Syndrome''' beforehand. and [[The Halloween Party]] both possess entire armies worth of '''Jovial''' soldiers. == The Last Ditch Beggar Trend == Recently, an incredibly disturbing trend has been circulating on [[Backchat (Collaborative Log)|Backchat]] involving those recently infected with [[Sanguine Festivus]] actively seeking out [[Caius Taggert]] and voluntarily joining his ranks. This trend stems from a belief that if they please him enough, he could use his control over the virus to artificially trigger '''Jovial Syndrome''' so that the victim is not doomed to a life of misery. In rare occurrences, it appears that Taggert has followed through with their wishes and granted his new followers eternal happiness. This has only strengthened the misinformation campaigns which Taggert is all to pleased to be involved in. These witness testimonies have been enough for many to believe that they can offer up their loyalty in return for unlimited happiness and a high ranking position in Taggert's army (which '''Jovials''' are awarded by default due to being smarter than the [[Sanguine Festivus|Partygoers]]). Very recently, even some uninfected humans have offered their loyalty in return for finding happiness. Some are desperate and have turned to '''Jovial Syndrome''' as a last resort. Some believe that they could resist Taggert's telepathic influence. They could not. Do not trust them. It is far more likely for you to be betrayed and end up another [[Sanguine Festivus|mindless cog]] in a war machine. {{Mail|Date Sent=22 January 2026|Sent By=[[Caius Taggert|your new best friend]]|Sent To={{USERNAME}}|Content=Well hello there pal! Don't know me? Ah, don't worry about it, 'cause I sure as hell know you! Couldn't help noticing ya back in [[The Hub]] yesterday. Ya seem really down in the dumps, kid! What's wrong? Job application got rejected by the [[M.E.G.|meggies]] again? Bet ya wonderin' how I know that one? Eh, don't worry 'bout it. I keep track on all my friends! Hey! I know! How 'bout I offer ya sumthin' ta put a smile on ya face again! I see y'already know 'bout it, I can see yer search history, after all. But the odds are so slim? Look at ya! Ya life's a living hell! What'cha got to lose? So come on! Where's the fun in life if ya never take a risk? Put it all on green! I'll be waiting!}}{{Collapse|num=2|final=
'''- Close Author & Licencing Information'''
